hero.title: Who Is Moe?
hero.subtitle: This is the space to get an initial look into who I am and if I'd be a good fit for you or your team!

designerSection.title: Who I Am As A Designer
paragraphs: Personality. We're losing it, and losing it fast! In a time when so much is automated and processes literally change between the time you clock in and clock out, how do we actually define what makes a good designer?

I believe that we're beginning to almost exclusively prioritize the how and the why. That's why the reason behind the design holds immense importance to me as a designer. Aside from the metrics and numbers (which are still important!) I'd like to think that placing more emphasis on the person sitting on the other side of the screen is what actually separates good design from great design.

Because at the end of the day, I'm not designing for dashboards or deliverables. I'm designing for the person who just had a long day and needs something to just work. For the person who feels overwhelmed and needs to feel guided. I got into this field because I love knowing that at the end of the day, I'm helping a real person, and I genuinely care about making their lives a little easier.

outsideDesignSection.title: Who I Am As Me
paragraphs: Summing myself up in a few sentences is tough, but I'll do my best. I've always carried a mix of analytical and creative energy. One side takes over in waves, but I always find a way to balance them back out. I have a natural curiosity for life, a desire to experience, understand, and learn. I lean extroverted day-to-day, but I can read a room and adapt without issue.

I love sports and video games. I've played badminton my whole life, but volleyball has been my main thing the past few years. On the gaming side, I grew up on Pokémon and have been playing since I was four. I have less time for these things now, which only makes the time I do get all the more valuable.
I'm still figuring a lot out about myself. I have a growth-oriented mindset and am looking for the right place to fulfill that growth.

valuesSection.title: Some of My Design Values
valuesSection.subtitle: If you'd like to know more about the way I work and how I fit with different project methodologies, check out my process.

values.name: Systems Thinking
values.description: I think in systems, not in individual designs. I build design systems with semantic token architecture, documented decisions, and room to scale. Every decision is part of a larger system between the design and the people who build and interact with it.

values.name: Product Design
values.description: End-to-end product design from concept to handoff. I translate ambiguous ideas into clear, user-testable product directions. I build interaction patterns, component libraries, and high-fidelity prototypes that developers can actually build from. My work survives contact with reality.

values.name: UX Research
values.description: Contextual interviews, usability testing, competitive audits, heuristic reviews, and synthesis. I start every project by understanding people and processes, not by working with assumptions.
